title: Exodus: Passover and Departure
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: passover and departure and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: passover and departure and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: Where was the blood of the Passover lamb to be applied, and what did it protect against? Answer: on the doorposts and lintel of each Israelite household, protecting them from the angel of death.
segments.correct_answer: on the doorposts and lintel, protecting the household from the angel of death
acceptable_variants: on the door frames of each house
on the lintel and two doorposts as protection from the destroyer
the blood marked each home so death passed over

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The annual Israelite feast commemorating God's sparing of the firstborn in Egypt; the blood of a lamb marked each household, and the meal was eaten in haste as Israel prepared to leave
The seven-day feast immediately following Passover in which no leaven was eaten, symbolizing the hasty departure from Egypt and separation from corruption
